_about: ICP for the *recipient* of the outbound (the buyer Daniel at Truelink is targeting), not the sender. Truelink is a fictional fraud-scoring / KYC API; its buyers are VPs of Risk and Heads of Compliance at payments fintechs and neobanks.

priorities: ship a measurable reduction in chargeback rate to the CFO before Q4 close
tune the fraud-rules engine without crushing approval rates on good customers
stand up authorised-push-payment-fraud defenses before the new liability rules bite
run incident postmortems that hold up in regulator review
painPoints: rules-vs-models false-positive tradeoff: tightening drops approval rates 4-7 points and Sales escalates within 48 hours; loosening lets through synthetic identities that surface as chargebacks 60 days later
vendor-stack opacity: KYC, device fingerprinting, behavioural, AML screening across five vendors with three different rule-engine syntaxes
model-drift detection is manual: someone runs a query every two weeks; by the time drift surfaces, two weeks of chargebacks have already shipped
executive reporting asks for a fraud-rate number that does not exist: declines? chargebacks? attempt-rate? loss as percent of GMV?

priorities: close 100% of regulator-flagged KYC gaps before the next audit cycle
cut customer-onboarding latency without dropping below the FinCEN-acceptable verification bar
automate the SAR drafting pipeline so analysts spend hours on real cases
consolidate three vendor portals into one operations dashboard the COO can read
painPoints: KYC-vendor-falloff: each vendor gives 85-92% verification pass-rate on its own; stacked in a waterfall the cumulative friction-driven abandon rate is 18-23%, and Sales blames Compliance every Monday
audit trail gaps: regulator asks who decided to onboard a flagged customer and the answer involves screenshots from three vendor portals stitched by a junior analyst at midnight
false-positive rate on enhanced due diligence: 60-70% of EDD cases close as no-action, so analyst hours go to confirming legitimate customers instead of chasing real bad actors
regulatory reporting cadence misalignment: SARs ship monthly per FinCEN cadence, but internal investigations close on a 90-day cycle, so reports lag the actual cases by two months
